name: Sweet Rice Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring water to a boil in a 2-quart saucepan.
2. Add rice and salt, cover, and reduce heat to medium.
3. Cook for one hour, checking if more water is needed to prevent sticking.
4. Remove from heat and let cool for 5 minutes.
5. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
6. Mix half the raisins and half the cinnamon into half of the rice and process thoroughly through a grain mill. Repeat with remaining rice, raisins, and cinnamon.
7. Place walnuts on a baking sheet and roast in the oven until cool; coarsely grind or finely chop the walnuts.
8. Measure rice into 2 1/2 tablespoon portions. Moisten hands and shape dumplings.
9. Roll each dumpling in walnuts to coat. Serve immediately.
Per 2 dumplings: 361 calories, 11 grams protein, 21 milligrams sodium, 38 grams carbohydrates, 21 grams fat, 0 grams cholesterol, 36 milligrams calcium
HINTS: To speed up the cooking process, place rice in a pressure cooker with 2 1/4 cups of water and cook for 40 to 45 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool until pressure goes down. Dumplings can be stored in the refrigerator for several days. Reheat in the oven when ready to serve.
